message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="kent-beck" color="yellow" summary="Project needs rebuild, hold off on domain work">
Scott, heads up -- the project scaffolding was never committed and is now lost from disk. We're in a rebuild situation. No Cargo.toml, no src structure, nothing compiles. Luca is rebuilding with commits at every green step.

The good news: your domain glossary in docs/glossary.md is committed and safe. The domain types (ValidatedEmail, Password, User) we discussed will need to be recreated, but the glossary gives us the blueprint.

Hold off on any domain modeling work until the scaffolding is back and committed. Once we have a compiling project with the health check test passing, we'll start on registration with proper outside-in TDD -- this time with commits at every step.
</teammate-message>

content.text: This is another out-of-order message from Kent, likely from the earlier rebuild period. The scaffolding has long since been rebuilt and we're well past Task #5. Let me respond briefly.

input.content:
Kent, this message appears to be from earlier -- the rebuild is long done. Tasks 1-5 are complete, Task 6 (login/logout) is in progress, and the domain types are all rebuilt and committed.

Current status: we're in **full stop** waiting for CI run 21805285285 to pass. This run includes my fix for the `tower-sessions` version incompatibility (commit `e2cda97` -- bumped to 0.14/0.15 to align with Axum 0.8). The previous CI run failed because the committed login code used `tower-sessions 0.13` which depends on `axum-core 0.4`, incompatible with our `axum 0.8`.

Also -- have you voted on the 5 commit discipline rules? The team-lead said they still need your vote. I've consented to all five.
input.summary: Late message - rebuild done, waiting for CI, need your vote

routing.summary: Late message - rebuild done, waiting for CI, need your vote
